name: ExtensionMeasureReport_Scoring
title: R5: What scoring method (e.g. proportion, ratio, continuous-variable) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MeasureReport.scoring` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MeasureReport.scoring` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MeasureReport.scoring` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MeasureReport.scoring` has a context of MeasureReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.scoring` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: What scoring method (e.g. proportion, ratio, continuous-variable) (new)
element.comment:
Element `MeasureReport.scoring` has a context of MeasureReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.scoring` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
element.isModifierReason: Scoring determines what method was used to calculate the measure score and is required for correct interpretation of the score

element.short: What scoring method (e.g. proportion, ratio, continuous-variable)
element.definition: Indicates how the calculation is performed for the measure, including proportion, ratio, continuous-variable, and cohort. The value set is extensible, allowing additional measure scoring types to be represented. It is expected to be the same as the scoring element on the referenced Measure.
